reviewer: solo new-family build 2026-07-21: real inference proven FIRST in headless Chrome (transformers.js audio-classification WASM q8), then the deployed page driven end-to-end. The model classifies the raw 16 kHz waveform into the 10 GTZAN genres; verified distinct from the built AudioSet AST + zero-shot CLAP. Built-in samples are procedural first-party clips (a chord progression -> jazz, a drum beat -> hiphop); real range comes from user upload.

rubric.evidence: Real inference verified in headless Chrome (transformers.js audio-classification, WASM q8) BEFORE building. The DistilHuBERT model classifies the raw 16 kHz waveform into the 10 GTZAN genres: existing instrumental piano clips -> classical 0.94-0.97 (correct); two procedural first-party samples read to DISTINCT sensible genres - a sustained chord progression -> jazz 0.85, a boom-bap drum beat -> hiphop 0.76. Deployed page (scripts/validate-music-genre-classification.mjs 9/9) shows the sample -> a top-genre verdict + the full 10-genre distribution (probabilities summing to 1). Every bar is the model's real softmax, not canned.
rubric.notes: DISTINCT from the built audio taggers: ast-audio-classification tags 527 AudioSet sound events; CLAP does zero-shot audio matching; neither yields a dedicated music-genre verdict.

rubric.evidence: Input a 16 kHz mono Float32 waveform (any uploaded/recorded file is decoded + resampled in the browser via OfflineAudioContext) -> softmax over 10 genres. A distinct audio capability + input from the built classifiers: it reads MUSIC and returns a genre distribution, not a transcript (ASR), an emotion (speech-emotion), or a general sound-event tag (AST).
rubric.notes: top_k:10 returns the whole label set so the confidence chart is complete.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the model (onnx-community/Musical-genres-Classification-Hubert-V1-ONNX, linked, ONNX build of SeyedAli/...-Hubert-V1), task (audio-classification), the 10 GTZAN genres, architecture (DistilHuBERT base ntu-spml/distilhubert), quantisation (q8), backend (WASM), input (16 kHz mono), download (~24 MB) and license (Apache-2.0). The How-it-works block shows the real pipeline call. No invented API.
rubric.notes: transformers.js is loaded via the shared lib/webai.js loadPipeline; the 10 labels come from the model config.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'onnx-community/Musical-genres-Classification-Hubert-V1-ONNX', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:24}). First visit shows an explicit Download (~24 MB); the ONNX + config are cached by transformers.js so a returning visit auto-initialises (checking -> ready) with NO Download click. Verified: fresh profile showed Download; clicking it enabled the controls.
rubric.notes: Small model, so re-download is cheap, but the never-silently-download guarantee + auto-init on return still hold via the shared loader; inference is off the main thread in a module Worker.

rubric.evidence: checking -> Download (~24 MB) -> ready (controls enabled) -> decoding -> listening -> verdict + bars. Decode/classification failures surface on the page in a role=status .err region (not only the console); the record button is disabled with a reason when the browser has no MediaRecorder. Verified download -> ready -> classify end-to-end.
rubric.notes: The clip info line shows the file name + duration; the waveform is drawn before classification so the user sees what was loaded.

rubric.evidence: Four real input paths: drag-and-drop, a Choose-file picker, an 8-second mic recorder (feature-detected), and two built-in sample chips. Every control is labelled and keyboard-operable; the drop zone gives visual drag feedback; no dead controls.
rubric.notes: Record is honestly gated on MediaRecorder support; drop rejects non-audio files with a readable message.

rubric.evidence: The verdict + all 10 bars are the model's real softmax; the readout shows the live backend + latency + clip duration. The page is HONEST about limits: a best-effort GTZAN model swayed by dominant texture (a chord progression reads jazz, a drum loop hip-hop), non-music (speech) gets the nearest genre, and the two procedural samples cover only jazz+hiphop - so the copy explicitly tells users to UPLOAD a real song for the full ten-genre range.
rubric.notes: No genre is hard-coded; the samples are procedural first-party clips whose classifications were verified, not asserted.

rubric.evidence: The verdict + status are role=status aria-live; the confidence bars are text (emoji + genre name + %), so colour is never the only signal; every button has an accessible name; the file input is a real labelled <input type=file>. The waveform canvas has an aria-label.
rubric.notes: Drag-drop has a keyboard-operable Choose-file equivalent; genre colour is decorative on top of the always-present text label + percentage.

rubric.evidence: The drop zone, tools, samples, waveform and bars all use fluid widths and wrap; the waveform canvas is width:100%. Verified 0 horizontal overflow at desktop AND mobile 360px (validate 9/9), 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: The confidence-bar grid (label / track / %) stays legible on narrow screens.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs in a dedicated module Worker; a DistilHuBERT forward pass on a short clip is fast on WASM, and the ~24 MB download is small + one-time (cached). Audio decode/resample uses OfflineAudioContext; the waveform is drawn once per clip.
rubric.notes: The audio Float32Array is transferred (not copied) to the worker.

guidanceConsulted.query: drag and drop file upload with record from microphone, accessible
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Offer multiple accessible input paths (a real file input + drag-drop + capture), give drag feedback, and feature-detect capture APIs rather than assuming them.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — a labelled <input type=file> + a Choose-file button + a drop zone with drag feedback + an 8s MicRecorder gated on MediaRecorder support (disabled with a reason when absent).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: four input paths; record honestly disabled without mic support; drop rejects non-audio

guidanceConsulted.query: decode and resample audio in the browser and keep the UI responsive
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Do heavy work (model inference) off the main thread; keep interactions responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — classification runs in a module Worker; audio decode/resample uses OfflineAudioContext and the PCM buffer is transferred to the worker.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: UI stays responsive; inference never blocks the main thread

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible results that don't rely on colour alone
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce dynamic results in an aria-live region and never encode meaning in colour alone.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the verdict is role=status aria-live; each genre bar shows emoji + name + percentage as text, with colour purely decorative.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: results readable as text; colour is redundant

openQuestions: Would averaging the genre distribution over several windows of a long track (instead of one clip) give a more stable verdict for full songs?
Is there a cleanly-licensed genre model trained on a larger, more modern corpus than GTZAN (which is small and dated) to widen real-world accuracy?
followUpGoals: Add a windowed 'genre over time' timeline for long uploads (reuse the audio-window pattern from the speech-emotion demo).
Offer a 'sort my files by genre' batch mode for multiple dropped tracks.
Source a couple of cleanly-licensed real-song samples (CC0) to widen the built-in sample range beyond jazz + hip-hop.
summary: Music genre classification (DistilHuBERT, GTZAN 10 genres) — a distinct audio family: a dedicated MUSIC-GENRE verdict + full 10-genre confidence chart from the raw waveform, all on-device, vs the built AudioSet AST (527 sound events) and zero-shot CLAP. Proven correct in the browser on transformers.js WASM q8 (piano->classical, chord progression->jazz, drum beat->hiphop). ~24 MB, Apache-2.0. Drop/choose/record/sample input; index demo complete + verified 9/9. Honest that it's a best-effort GTZAN model and that the procedural samples (jazz+hiphop) are limited - upload a real song for the full range. A genre-over-time timeline, batch sort, and CC0 real-song samples are recorded follow-ups.
